content:
Day 21
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "She was on a pilgrimage to the dying Buddha, with her son... Near the ferry... Kamala, who was also tired, lay down... He saw a black snake and ran away screaming."
  • Today's Intention: I will reflect on how paths can cross again in unexpected ways and be open to these synchronicities.
NIGHT
  • Night Reading: "Siddhartha knew it was she, and he felt as if his heart were breaking. He bent over her face, which was still beautiful though it was pale, and read in it her past life."
  • Reflection: Reviewing the week, how has the theme of "listening" changed my perspective? What did I hear this week that I might have missed before?
Week 4: Love, Loss, and Unity
Weekly Goals:
  • Goal 1:
  • Goal 2:
  • Goal 3:
Day 22
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "She died, and Siddhartha saw her face grow pale... Her face was now no more than the face of any woman who has just died, and her body was the body of any woman who has just died."
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 22 (chunk 22/29)
